---
title: "Calculate Length at Maturity from Size at Age Data"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

## Load in Data

```{r}
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
data("cs_age_size")
```

## Calculate Length at Maturity

```{r}
# Filter to include only 'immature' and 'mature'
maturity_testing_filtered <- cs_age_size%>%
    filter(Maturity %in% c("immature", "mature"))%>%
    mutate(Maturity=ifelse(Maturity == "mature", 1, 0))%>%
    uncount(CANoAtLngt) %>%
    mutate(CANoAtLngt = 1)

# Remove rows where LngtClass is NA or not finite
maturity_testing_filtered <- maturity_testing_filtered %>%
  filter(!is.na(LngtClass), is.finite(LngtClass), !is.na(Maturity))

#species remaining after filtering
unique(maturity_testing_filtered$Scientific_name)
#"Merluccius merluccius"      "Chelidonichthys cuculus"    "Gadus morhua"               #"Lepidorhombus whiffiagonis""Melanogrammus aeglefinus"   "Merlangius merlangus"       #"Pleuronectes platessa"      "Scomber scombrus"         "Solea solea"

#Linear model
fit <- glm(Maturity ~ LngtClass*Scientific_name, family = binomial, data = maturity_testing_filtered)
coefs <- coef(fit)
#Coefs are in odd order to extract LM of each species 
#need to put negative intercept over slope
lm<-c(
-coefs[1]/coefs[2],
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[3]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[11]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[4]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[12]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[5]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[13]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[6]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[14]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[7]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[15]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[8]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[16]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[9]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[17]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[10]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[18]))

lm_fin <- data.frame(
  Scientific_name = (c("Chelidonichthys cuculus","Gadus morhua","Lepidorhombus whiffiagonis",
                       "Melanogrammus aeglefinus","Merlangius merlangus",
                       "Merluccius merluccius","Pleuronectes platessa","Scomber scombrus",
                       "Solea solea")),
  l_mat = as.numeric(lm),
  row.names = NULL
)

l_mat<-lm_fin

library(ggplot2)

# Base plot with points
ggplot(maturity_testing_filtered, aes(x = LngtClass, y = Maturity, color = Scientific_name)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.05, width = 0.1, alpha = 0.5) +  # jitter for binary points
  # Add logistic regression curves for each species
  stat_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"), se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = "Length class",
    y = "Probability of being mature",
    color = "Species",
    title = "Maturity vs Length by Species"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Calculate Age at Maturity

```{r}
# Filter to include only 'immature' and 'mature'
maturity_testing_filtered <- cs_age_size%>%
    filter(Maturity %in% c("immature", "mature"))%>%
    mutate(Maturity=ifelse(Maturity == "mature", 1, 0))%>%
    uncount(CANoAtLngt) %>%
    mutate(CANoAtLngt = 1)

# Remove rows where LngtClass is NA or not finite
maturity_testing_filtered <- maturity_testing_filtered %>%
  filter(!is.na(LngtClass), is.finite(LngtClass), !is.na(Maturity), !is.na(Age))

#species remaining after filtering
unique(maturity_testing_filtered$Scientific_name)

#Linear model
fit <- glm(Maturity ~ Age*Scientific_name, family = binomial, 
           data = maturity_testing_filtered)
coefs <- coef(fit)
#Coefs are in odd order to extract LM of each species 
#need to put negative intercept over slope
lm<-c(
-coefs[1]/coefs[2],
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[3]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[11]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[4]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[12]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[5]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[13]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[6]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[14]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[7]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[15]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[8]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[16]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[9]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[17]),
-1*(coefs[1]+coefs[10]) / (coefs[2]+coefs[18]))

lm_fin <- data.frame(
  Scientific_name = (c("Chelidonichthys cuculus","Gadus morhua",
                       "Lepidorhombus whiffiagonis", 
                       "Melanogrammus aeglefinus", "Merlangius merlangus",
                       "Merluccius merluccius","Pleuronectes platessa",
                       "Scomber scombrus","Solea solea")),
  age_mat = as.numeric(lm),
  row.names = NULL
)
age_mat<-lm_fin

# Base plot with points
ggplot(maturity_testing_filtered, aes(x = Age, y = Maturity, color = Scientific_name)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.05, width = 0.1, alpha = 0.5) +  # jitter for binary points
  # Add logistic regression curves for each species
  stat_smooth(method = "glm", method.args = list(family = "binomial"), se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    x = "Age",
    y = "Probability of being mature",
    color = "Species",
    title = "Maturity vs Length by Species"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

# Combine Parameters into single data frame

```{r}
cs_calculated_histories<-l_mat%>%
    left_join(age_mat, by="Scientific_name")
use_data(cs_calculated_histories,overwrite=TRUE)
```

# ML is sensitive try a Biased reduced approach with confidence intervals via bootstrapping

```{r}
#Model
fit <- glm(Maturity ~ Age * Scientific_name,
             family = binomial(link = "logit"),
             data = maturity_testing_filtered,
             method = "brglmFit",
             type = "AS_mixed")
#Predict
pred <- maturity_testing_filtered %>%
    group_by(Scientific_name) %>%
    summarise(
        minL = min(Age),
        maxL = max(Age),
        .groups = "drop") %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(Age = list(seq(minL, maxL, by = 0.1))) %>%
    unnest(Age)
 
pred$p <- predict(fit, newdata = pred, type = "response")

L50 <- pred %>%
    group_by(Scientific_name) %>%
    slice(which.min(abs(p - 0.5))) %>%
    select(Scientific_name, L50 = Age)

L50

#Plot
ggplot(pred, aes(Age, p, colour = Scientific_name)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed") +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(y = "Probability mature", x = "Length class (cm)", title = "Species-specific maturity ogives")

#Bootstrap for CI
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
nboot <- 200   # number of bootstrap samples

# Create a list to store bootstrap L50 results
boot_L50 <- vector("list", nboot)

for(i in 1:nboot) {
    #Resample each species with replacement
    boot_data <- maturity_testing_filtered %>%
        group_by(Scientific_name) %>%
        sample_frac(replace = TRUE) %>%
        ungroup()
    
    #Fit bias-reduced GLM with species-specific slopes
    fit_boot <- glm(
        Maturity ~ Age * Scientific_name,
        family = binomial(link = "logit"),
        data = boot_data,
        method = "brglmFit",
        type = "AS_mixed")
    
    #Create length sequence per species for prediction
    pred_boot <- boot_data %>%
        group_by(Scientific_name) %>%
        summarise(
            minL = min(Age),
            maxL = max(Age),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
        rowwise() %>%
        mutate(Age = list(seq(minL, maxL, by = 0.1))) %>%
        unnest(Age)
    
    #Predict probability of maturity
    pred_boot$p <- predict(fit_boot, newdata = pred_boot, type = "response")
    
    #Calculate L50 (length at 50% maturity) per species
    L50_boot <- pred_boot %>%
        group_by(Scientific_name) %>%
        slice(which.min(abs(p - 0.5))) %>%
        select(Scientific_name, L50 = Age)
    
    boot_L50[[i]] <- L50_boot
}

#Combine bootstrap results into a single dataframe
boot_L50_df <- bind_rows(boot_L50, .id = "boot")
 
#Compute 95% confidence intervals for L50
L50_CI <- boot_L50_df %>%
     group_by(Scientific_name) %>%
     summarise(
         L50_median = median(L50),
         L50_lower = quantile(L50, 0.025),
         L50_upper = quantile(L50, 0.975),
         .groups = "drop"
     )

# A tibble: 9 × 4
#  Scientific_name            L50_median L50_lower L50_upper
#  <chr>                           <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>
#1 Chelidonichthys cuculus          21.3      20.9      21.7
#2 Gadus morhua                     42.3      34.4      47.1
#3 Lepidorhombus whiffiagonis       20.6      20.5      20.8
#4 Melanogrammus aeglefinus         23.5      21.8      25  
#5 Merlangius merlangus             21.1      20.7      21.5
#6 Merluccius merluccius            32.9      31.5      34.3
#7 Pleuronectes platessa            17.7      15.5      19.1
#8 Scomber scombrus                 28.1      27.1      29.1
#9 Solea solea                      20.8      19.7      21.8
```
